Title: 4A.4: Go Live
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Execute the go-live: publish the Hop Lun website to production, confirm everything is working on the live domain, and monitor for immediate issues in the first hour post-launch. This task is short in duration but high in consequence — the team must be available and ready to act during the launch window.

**Scope:**

* Publish site to production in Webflow
* Confirm live domain is resolving correctly and SSL is active
* Post-launch smoke test across key pages and functionality
* Notify client site is live
* Monitor for the first hour post-launch

**Output:** Hop Lun website live on production domain. All key functionality confirmed working. Client notified. PM closes the launch.

**Success Metric:** Site live within agreed window. All critical paths working (homepage, forms, CMS pages). No P0 issues in first hour. Client receives launch notification.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Reference Materials:**

* Pre-launch checklist completed (4A.3): \[link\]
* Production domain: \[CLIENT-DOMAIN\].com
* Webflow publish settings: \[Webflow project link\]
* Go-live window agreed with client: \[DATE AND TIME from 4A.3\]

**Go-Live Sequence:**

1. Confirm DNS has propagated (use https://whatsmydns.net to check)
2. Confirm SSL certificate is active (padlock icon in browser)
3. Publish site in Webflow (Settings → Hosting → Publish to production)
4. Run smoke test (see acceptance criteria)
5. Confirm GA4 is tracking live traffic
6. Notify client (Slack, email, or agreed method)
7. Monitor for 1 hour post-launch

**Smoke Test Pages (minimum):**

* Homepage — loads, hero animation, nav links
* One Solutions page — content, CTA
* Careers listing — CMS items showing
* Get a Quote — form submits, email received
* 404 — invalid URL triggers custom 404 page
* Mobile — homepage and form on mobile device (real device preferred)

**Post-Launch Monitoring:**

* GA4 Realtime view — confirm live traffic appearing
* Search Console — no immediate crawl errors
* Browser console on key pages — no JavaScript errors
* Form submissions — confirm emails still routing correctly on live domain

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Pre-Go-Live Checks:**

- [ ] DNS fully propagated (confirmed via whatsmydns.net — all regions green)
- [ ] SSL certificate active on production domain (https:// resolving, no warnings)
- [ ] Webflow published to production (not just staging)
- [ ] www and non-www both resolving to correct domain (redirect in place)

**Smoke Test (Post-Publish):**

- [ ] Homepage loads on live domain: hero animation, nav, all sections visible
- [ ] Navigation: all main nav links working, dropdowns functional
- [ ] Solutions page: loads correctly, Get a Quote CTA linked
- [ ] Careers listing: active jobs displaying, job detail template loading
- [ ] Resources: News listing page and one article template loading correctly
- [ ] Get a Quote form: submits successfully on live domain (email received at correct address)
- [ ] Contact Us form: submits successfully on live domain
- [ ] 404 page: confirmed triggering on invalid URL
- [ ] Mobile: homepage and Get a Quote form tested on real mobile device

**Analytics:**

- [ ] GA4 Realtime view showing live traffic within 5 minutes of publish
- [ ] A form submission test on live domain: event appearing in GA4

**Client Notification:**

- [ ] Client notified: site is live (via agreed channel — Slack, email)
- [ ] Live URL confirmed and shared with client
- [ ] Brief note on what to expect in the next 48 hours (DNS fully settled, Search Console indexing)

**Post-Launch Monitoring (1 hour):**

- [ ] No P0 issues reported or detected in first hour
- [ ] GA4 Realtime showing expected traffic patterns
- [ ] No critical errors in browser console on key pages
- [ ] If P0 issue detected: team responds immediately, rollback to staging if needed

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* DNS not yet propagated — wait and re-check, do not publish until DNS is confirmed
* SSL certificate not yet issued — wait up to 48 hours after DNS change, do not go live on http
* Webflow publish fails — check Webflow status page, contact Webflow support if persistent
* P0 issue discovered during smoke test — fix immediately or rollback to staging, do not leave live with a broken critical path

## Task Dropoff

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders when all the acceptance criteria is completed. If needed, mark the task as **In Review**.*

* Live URL confirmed: \[CLIENT-DOMAIN\].com
* Smoke test results: all items checked and passing: \[screenshot or checklist\]
* GA4 Realtime screenshot (traffic showing): \[screenshot\]
* Client notification sent: \[screenshot or message link\]
* Post-launch monitoring note: "No P0 issues detected in first hour" (or issues logged if found)
* Launch timestamp: \[date and time site went live\]

**Mark task as "Complete"** — no review needed, PM confirms live status directly. Proceed immediately to 4A.5 Client Training.

* Priority: Urgent

* Estimate: 4 points

The core objectives are to elevate the client's brand, implement a scalable CMS, and boost commercial and HR engagement.
* Depends on: HPL-4A3 Launch Preparation complete and PM sign-off received

* Due Date: Week 16 (go-live date TBC with client)

Title: 4A.1: UAT Management
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Coordinate and manage the client User Acceptance Testing (UAT) process. Prepare UAT materials, provide client access to staging, collect and categorise all feedback, and ensure all P0 and P1 issues are resolved before launch. UAT is the client's formal sign-off on the build — it ends with written approval to go live.

**Scope:**

* UAT scenario document prepared (20-25 test cases)
* Client stakeholders given staging access and briefed on process
* Feedback collected, logged in Linear, and categorised
* All P0/P1 issues resolved and re-verified
* Written client approval received (Gate G5)

**Output:** Written client approval to proceed to launch. All P0/P1 issues closed. P2/P3 issues logged for post-launch or acknowledged.

**Success Metric:** Client provides written approval within the UAT window. <5 P0/P1 issues raised (indicating quality internal QA in 3A.11). Zero P0 issues remaining at close of UAT.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Reference Materials:**

* Staging URL (full site): \[staging link\]
* Approved designs (Phase 2B): \[Figma link\]
* Internal QA report (Phase 3A.11): \[Linear link\]

**UAT Scenario Areas (20-25 tests):**

* Homepage journey (hero CTA, nav, scroll behaviour)
* About Us section (Leadership, Our Story, Locations listing and template)
* Solutions section (all 5 pages, CTAs linked correctly)
* Sustainability section (all 3 pages)
* Careers (listing, active/inactive toggle, job detail template)
* Resources & Insights (all 3 listings, article templates, is_gated logic)
* Contact section (all 4 forms — submit and confirm email received)
* Utility pages (Privacy, Terms, 404)
* Mobile experience (key pages on actual device, not just DevTools)
* Analytics tracking (confirm page views and events firing)

**Client Stakeholders for UAT:**

* Client Sponsor: \[NAME\] — strategic approval
* Client PM: \[NAME\] — day-to-day testing
* Others: \[LIST if known\]

**Issue Resolution SLA:**

* P0 (Critical — broken functionality): Fix and re-verify before UAT closes
* P1 (High — visual or functional issue): Fix within same UAT cycle
* P2 (Medium): Fix before launch if time allows, otherwise post-launch
* P3 (Low): Document for post-launch, no commitment

**Scope Boundary:**

* UAT is for testing what was built against the approved design and SOW
* New feature or design change requests from UAT → log as change request, do not action without PM approval

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**UAT Preparation:**

- [ ] UAT scenario document created (20-25 test cases covering all sections)
- [ ] Staging URL confirmed live and accessible
- [ ] Client stakeholders given staging access
- [ ] UAT briefing session held or briefing document sent (what to test, how to log feedback, deadline)
- [ ] Feedback collection method confirmed (Linear, Figma comments, doc, or email)
- [ ] UAT window communicated: start date, feedback deadline, resolution window

**UAT Execution (Client-Led):**

- [ ] Client completes UAT scenario checklist
- [ ] All feedback collected by deadline
- [ ] All feedback logged in Linear within 24 hours of receipt
- [ ] Each item categorised: In-scope vs out-of-scope, P0/P1/P2/P3
- [ ] Out-of-scope or new requests flagged to PM for change request discussion

**Issue Resolution:**

- [ ] All P0 issues resolved and re-tested
- [ ] All P1 issues resolved and re-tested
- [ ] P2 issues triaged: fixed or accepted as post-launch
- [ ] P3 issues logged in Linear with "Post-Launch" label
- [ ] Client notified of all resolutions and any items deferred to post-launch

**Client Sign-Off (Gate G5):**

- [ ] Final walkthrough conducted with client (or async confirmation)
- [ ] Client provides written approval to proceed to launch (email or signed doc)
- [ ] Approval documented and saved to project folder
- [ ] Gate G5 checklist complete

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* Internal QA (3A.11) not complete — do not invite client to UAT until P0/P1 issues are resolved internally
* Client stakeholders unavailable during UAT window — chase early, escalate to account if no response within 2 days
* Client raises scope change during UAT — do not action, log as change request and flag to PM immediately
* P0 issues taking longer than expected to fix — communicate timeline impact to PM, may need to extend UAT window
* Client slow to give written approval — send a clear deadline, escalate to account if overdue

## Task Dropoff

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders when all the acceptance criteria is completed. If needed, mark the task as **In Review**.*

* UAT scenario document: \[link\]
* UAT feedback log in Linear (all items, categorised): \[link to Linear view\]
* Issue resolution summary: P0: X (all resolved), P1: X (all resolved), P2: X (fixed or deferred), P3: X (post-launch): \[doc or comment\]
* Client written approval (email or signed doc): \[link\]
* Gate G5 completion confirmation: \[comment or doc\]
* Change requests raised (if any): \[Linear links\]

**Mark task as "In Review"** and tag PM to confirm Gate G5 is achieved. Once approved, proceed to 4A.2 Performance Optimisation and 4A.3 Launch Preparation in parallel.

## Metadata

* URL: \[To be assigned by Linear\]
* Identifier: HPL-4A1
* Status: To Do
* Priority: Urgent (Gates launch)
* Assignee: Unassigned
* Labels: Phase-4, UAT, Client-Review, Gate-G5
* Sprint: Cycle 8 (Week 14-15: Apr 21 - May 2, 2026)
* Estimate: 10 points
* Project: [[ARCHIVED] HPL-BetaEngagement-RMD](https://linear.app/admiralhq/project/hpl-betaengagement-rmd-6e8feb10977a). 16-week project for Hop Lun (HK) Ltd to deliver a high-performance Webflow website redesign. The core objectives are to elevate the client's brand, implement a scalable CMS, and boost commercial and HR engagement.
* Depends on: HPL-3A11 Responsive QA complete and signed off by PM
* Blocks: HPL-4A3 Launch Preparation (cannot finalise launch until written approval received)

Title: 3A.1a: Global Styles & Variables
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Map the approved design system (from Phase 2B.1) into Webflow variables and global styles. This establishes the single source of truth for all visual properties across the build — colors, typography, spacing, shadows, and transitions. Every component and page built in Phase 3 will reference these variables, so accuracy here prevents rework throughout the entire build.

**Scope:**

* Color variables for all design system tokens
* Typography styles for all text levels
* Spacing scale as variables
* Border radius, shadow styles, transition speeds

**Output:** All design system tokens live in Webflow as variables and styles, spot-checked against Figma handoff specs, ready for component and page builds.

**Success Metric:** Zero hardcoded values used in the build — everything references a variable. Design team spot-checks and confirms visual accuracy against Figma.

## Additional Notes

> *List any additional resources/materials here that would be helpful to complete the task*

**Reference Materials:**

* Design system (Phase 2B.1): \[Figma link\]
* Dev handoff package (Phase 2B.7): \[Google Drive link\]
* Webflow variables documentation: https://university.webflow.com/lesson/variables

**Design Tokens to Map:**

**Colors:**

* Primary: #FFB2E0 (Hop Lun Pink) + 5 shades
* Secondary: #F1D66F (Hop Lun Yellow) + 5 shades
* Tertiary: #F8F1EB (Cream) + variations
* Neutral: #A5AAB8 (Grey) + 10 shades (white to black)
* Semantic: Success, Warning, Error, Info

**Typography:**

* Font families: \[from handoff doc\]
* Text levels: H1, H2, H3, H4, H5, H6, Body L, Body M, Body S, Caption, Label
* Per level: font family, size, weight, line height, letter spacing

**Spacing Scale (8px base unit):**

* 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128px

**Other Tokens:**

* Border radius: none, sm (4px), md (8px), lg (16px), xl (24px), full (999px)
* Shadows: elevation-1 through elevation-5
* Transitions: fast (150ms), default (250ms), slow (400ms) — ease-in-out

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Color Variables:**

- [ ] All brand color variables created (primary, secondary, tertiary, neutral palettes)
- [ ] All shades created per palette (5 shades minimum per brand color)
- [ ] Semantic colors created (success, warning, error, info)
- [ ] Variable naming matches Figma token naming convention

**Typography Styles:**

- [ ] All text styles created for H1–H6
- [ ] Body styles created (L, M, S variants)
- [ ] Caption and Label styles created
- [ ] Each style has correct font family, size, weight, line height, and letter spacing
- [ ] Client brand fonts confirmed rendering correctly in browser (not fallback font)

**Spacing Variables:**

- [ ] Full spacing scale created as variables (4px through 128px)
- [ ] Variable naming is consistent and intuitive (e.g., space-xs, space-sm, space-md)

**Other Token Variables:**

- [ ] Border radius values created as variables
- [ ] Shadow styles created (elevation-1 through elevation-5)
- [ ] Transition speed variables defined (fast, default, slow)

**Quality Check:**

- [ ] All styles spot-checked in browser preview against Figma handoff specs
- [ ] No hardcoded hex values or pixel values in the styles panel
- [ ] Design team confirms visual accuracy before this sub-issue is closed

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* Figma tokens not clearly named or exported — reference handoff doc from 2B.7 or flag to design team
* Client brand fonts not rendering correctly — check font file format, confirm web licensing
* Token naming inconsistency between Figma and Webflow — align on convention before creating all variables

## Task Dropoff

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders when all the acceptance criteria is completed. If needed, mark the task as **In Review**.*

* Screenshot or Loom of Webflow variables panel (all variables created): \[link\]
* Screenshot of typography styles panel in Webflow: \[link\]
* Browser preview screenshot spot-checked against Figma: \[link\]
* Any deviations from design system tokens noted: \[comment or doc\]

**Mark task as "In Review"** and tag design team to confirm visual accuracy. Once approved, mark complete.

Title: 3A.1b: CMS Architecture
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Build and configure all Webflow CMS collections for the Hop Lun website. Five collections are required based on the approved sitemap. Each collection must have the correct fields, field types, and references before any content is entered or templates are built. Getting the architecture right now prevents costly restructuring later in the build.

**Scope:**

* 5 collections: Locations, Careers, News, Insights, Industry Reports
* All fields configured with correct types (text, rich text, image, toggle, option, etc.)
* Sample content added to each collection for template testing
* Reference fields connected where applicable

**Output:** All 5 CMS collections live in Webflow with correct fields, sample content populated, reviewed and approved by PM before any CMS template builds begin.

**Success Metric:** PM and dev team review all collections and confirm field structure is correct and complete. No field changes needed after template builds start.

## Additional Notes

> *List any additional resources/materials here that would be helpful to complete the task*

**Reference Materials:**

* Approved sitemap (Phase 2 Discovery): \[link\]
* Wireframes for CMS pages: \[Figma link\]
* Dev handoff package (Phase 2B.7): \[Google Drive link\]
* Webflow CMS documentation: https://university.webflow.com/lesson/intro-to-the-cms

**CMS Collections & Fields:**

**Locations** (About Us → Locations)

* name (plain text, required)
* country (plain text, required)
* region (plain text)
* address (plain text)
* photo (image)
* map_embed_url (plain text)
* is_headquarters (toggle)

**Careers** (top-level CMS)

* title (plain text, required)
* department (option: Design, Production, Operations, Sales, HR, Other)
* location (plain text, required)
* type (option: Full-time, Part-time, Contract)
* description (rich text, required)
* apply_url (link, required)
* is_active (toggle, required)

**News** (Resources & Insights → News)

* title (plain text, required)
* slug (plain text, required)
* featured_image (image, required)
* summary (plain text, required — used for cards and meta descriptions)
* body (rich text, required)
* publish_date (date, required)
* category (option: Company News, Industry News, Press Release)
* is_featured (toggle)

**Insights** (Resources & Insights → Insights)

* title (plain text, required)
* slug (plain text, required)
* featured_image (image, required)
* summary (plain text, required)
* body (rich text, required)
* publish_date (date, required)
* author (plain text)
* tags (multi-option)
* is_featured (toggle)

**Industry Reports** (Resources & Insights → Industry Reports)

* title (plain text, required)
* slug (plain text, required)
* cover_image (image, required)
* summary (plain text, required)
* file_download_url (link)
* publish_date (date, required)
* tags (multi-option)
* is_gated (toggle — controls whether download requires form submission)

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Collections Created:**

- [ ] Locations collection created with all fields
- [ ] Careers collection created with all fields
- [ ] News collection created with all fields
- [ ] Insights collection created with all fields
- [ ] Industry Reports collection created with all fields

**Field Configuration:**

- [ ] All required fields marked as required in Webflow
- [ ] Option fields have correct options pre-populated (department, type, category)
- [ ] Toggle fields default to correct state (is_active, is_featured default to off)
- [ ] Slug fields configured for SEO-friendly URL structure
- [ ] No unnecessary fields added beyond what's specified

**Sample Content:**

- [ ] 3 sample items added to each collection (15 items total)
- [ ] Sample content is realistic (not lorem ipsum) — use placeholder but plausible data
- [ ] Sample images added (can use placeholder images sized correctly)
- [ ] Sample content used to verify template designs render correctly

**Review & Approval:**

- [ ] All 5 collections reviewed by PM
- [ ] Field structure confirmed complete (no missing fields identified after review)
- [ ] PM approval received before any CMS template builds begin

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* Field requirements unclear from wireframes or handoff — flag to PM before building, don't assume
* is_gated toggle logic for Industry Reports requires form integration — confirm approach with PM before template build
* Slug structure not defined — confirm URL pattern with PM (e.g., /resources/news/\[slug\] vs /news/\[slug\])
* Option field values incomplete — confirm all dropdown options with PM before locking

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders when all the acceptance criteria is completed. If needed, mark the task as **In Review**.*

* Screenshot of all 5 collections in Webflow CMS panel: \[link\]
* Screenshot of each collection's field list: \[link or Loom walkthrough\]
* Confirmation that sample content is populated and rendering: \[staging link\]
* Any field decisions or assumptions documented: \[comment or doc\]

**Mark task as "In Review"** and tag PM to review collections before CMS template builds start. PM must approve before this is marked complete.

Title: 3A.1c: Core Symbols & Utility Classes
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Build the reusable Webflow symbols and utility class library that every page in the build will depend on. Core symbols (Header, Footer, Forms, CTA Band) are built once and used everywhere — getting them right saves significant time across the entire Phase 3 build. Utility classes establish the layout and spacing system that keeps the codebase clean and consistent.

**Scope:**

* 4 core symbols: Header/Nav, Footer, Form components, CTA Band
* Utility class library following Client-First methodology
* Interactions library (page load, scroll triggers, hover states, mobile menu, Lottie triggers)

**Output:** All symbols responsive and tested at 3 breakpoints. Utility classes documented. Interactions working and confirmed in staging. Build team can start page builds immediately after sign-off.

**Success Metric:** No one-off nav or footer builds across any page — all pages reference the same symbols. Interactions tested in browser with no console errors.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Reference Materials:**

* Component library (Phase 2B.2): \[Figma link\]
* Core page designs (Phase 2B.3): \[Figma link\]
* Animation documentation (Phase 2B.5): \[doc link\]
* Lottie animation files (Phase 2B.5): \[Google Drive folder\]
* Dev handoff package (Phase 2B.7): \[Google Drive link\]
* Client-First documentation: \[link\]

**Naming Convention (Client-First):**

* Utility class prefix: `u-`
* Component class prefix: `c-`
* Page-specific prefix: `p-`
* Wrapper class: `[component]_wrapper`
* Item class: `[component]_item`

**Symbol Specs (from Figma component library):**

* Header: Logo SVG, primary nav links, mobile hamburger trigger, sticky behavior
* Footer: 3-4 column layout, nav links, social icons, legal links, copyright — collapses to stack on mobile
* Forms: Contact form + Quote form + Request Samples form — all with validation states and success messages
* CTA Band: Heading, subtext, primary CTA button, optional secondary link — 3 background variants (light, dark, brand)

**Interactions to Configure:**

* Page load: subtle fade-in (opacity 0 → 1, 300ms)
* Scroll-triggered: fade-in + slide-up for content sections (trigger at 20% in view)
* Button hover: defined in component styles (color shift + shadow)
* Card hover: lift effect (translateY -4px, shadow elevation increase)
* Mobile menu: slide-in from right, overlay background
* Lottie triggers: hero animation on page load, icon animations on hover

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Header/Navigation Symbol:**

- [ ] Logo (SVG) placed and linked to homepage
- [ ] All primary nav links added and functional (Home, About, Solutions, Sustainability, Careers, Resources, Contact)
- [ ] Dropdown configured for Solutions sub-nav (Design Studio, Material Innovation, Product Development, Manufacturing)
- [ ] Dropdown configured for About sub-nav (Leadership, Our Story, Locations)
- [ ] Mobile hamburger trigger connected to mobile menu open/close interaction
- [ ] Sticky behavior configured (nav stays visible on scroll)
- [ ] Responsive layout tested: Desktop (full nav), Tablet (condensed), Mobile (hamburger only)

**Footer Symbol:**

- [ ] All column links added (mirrors sitemap structure)
- [ ] Social media icons linked (placeholder URLs — client to provide)
- [ ] Legal links present (Privacy Policy, Terms of Use)
- [ ] Copyright text with current year
- [ ] Responsive layout tested: Desktop (columns), Tablet (2 columns), Mobile (stacked)

**Form Components Symbol:**

- [ ] Contact form: name, company, email, phone, message fields — all with labels and placeholder text
- [ ] Get a Quote form: name, company, email, product type, volume, timeline, budget, notes
- [ ] Request Samples form: name, company, email, product type, sample quantity, shipping address
- [ ] Book a Factory Tour form: name, company, email, preferred date, group size, notes
- [ ] All forms have validation states styled (error highlight, success message)
- [ ] All form success messages configured ("Thank you, we'll be in touch")
- [ ] Forms tested: submit triggers success state, required fields show error if empty

**CTA Band Symbol:**

- [ ] Heading, subtext, and primary CTA button present
- [ ] Optional secondary link present
- [ ] 3 background variants built (light, dark, brand color)
- [ ] Responsive layout tested at all 3 breakpoints

**Utility Classes:**

- [ ] Spacing utilities built (u-margin-top-, u-padding-, common combinations)
- [ ] Text utilities built (u-text-center, u-text-uppercase, u-text-nowrap)
- [ ] Layout utilities built (u-container, u-grid-2col, u-grid-3col, u-flex-center)
- [ ] Visibility utilities built (u-hide-tablet, u-hide-mobile, u-show-mobile)

Title: 3A.1: Webflow Foundation Setup
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Set up the complete Webflow project foundation before any page builds begin. This includes configuring the project, environments, hosting, global styles (mapped from the design system), CMS architecture, core symbols, and utility classes. Everything built in Phase 3 depends on this foundation being correct — rework here is expensive, so quality matters more than speed.

**Scope:**

* Webflow project created and configured
* Environments set up (Development, Staging, Production)
* Global styles mapped from Phase 2 design system (variables, typography, colors, spacing)
* CMS collections defined with correct fields and references
* Core symbols built (Header, Footer, Form components, CTA sections)
* Utility classes and interactions library configured

**Output:** A fully configured Webflow project ready for page builds. Dev team can start building pages immediately after this task is complete.

**Success Metric:** All global styles match design system tokens exactly. CMS architecture reviewed and approved before content entry begins. Core symbols reused across pages (no one-off builds).

## Additional Notes

**Reference Materials:**

* Design system (Phase 2B.1): \[Figma link\]
* Component library (Phase 2B.2): \[Figma link\]
* Dev handoff package (Phase 2B.7): \[Google Drive link\]
* Approved wireframes and page designs: \[Figma link\]
* Client brand font files: `/01_CLIENT_PROVIDED/Brand Assets/Fonts/`

**Design System Tokens to Map into Webflow Variables:**

* Colors: Primary #FFB2E0, Secondary #F1D66F, Tertiary #F8F1EB, Neutral #A5AAB8 + all shades
* Typography: All text styles (H1-H6, Body, Caption) with font family, size, weight, line height
* Spacing: Base unit and full scale (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128px)
* Border radius values, shadow styles, transition speeds

**CMS Collections Required (5 collections from sitemap):**

* Locations — under About Us (name, country, region, address, photo, map embed, is_headquarters toggle)
* Careers — top-level (title, department, location, type, description, apply URL, is_active toggle)
* News — under Resources & Insights (title, slug, featured image, summary, body, publish date, category, is_featured toggle)
* Insights — under Resources & Insights (title, slug, featured image, summary, body, publish date, tags, author, is_featured toggle)
* Industry Reports — under Resources & Insights (title, slug, cover image, summary, file download URL, publish date, tags, is_gated toggle)

**Client-First Naming Convention:**

* All Webflow classes must follow Client-First methodology
* Reference: \[Client-First documentation link\]
* Utility class prefix: `u-`
* Component class prefix: `c-`
* Page-specific prefix: `p-`

**Hosting & Environment Setup:**

* Webflow project: \[PROJECT_NAME\]
* Hosting plan: \[PLAN_TYPE\]
* Staging URL: staging.\[CLIENT-DOMAIN\].com
* Production domain connection happens only after client go-live approval
* DNS provider: \[PROVIDER\] (credentials in 1Password: \[VAULT_NAME\])

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Project & Environment Setup:**

- [ ] Webflow project created with correct project name and team workspace
- [ ] Hosting plan configured
- [ ] Staging environment active and accessible

**Global Styles (Design System → Webflow Variables):**

- [ ] Color variables created for all design system colors (naming matches Figma tokens)
- [ ] Typography styles created for all text levels (H1-H6, Body L/M/S, Caption, Label)

- [ ] Border radius values set as variables
- [ ] Shadow styles created (elevation 1-5)

**CMS Architecture:**

- [ ] All 5 collections created (Locations, Careers, News, Insights, Industry Reports)

  * Locations: name, country, region, address, photo, map embed URL, is_headquarters (toggle)
  * Careers: title, department (option), location, type (full-time/part-time/contract), description (rich text), apply URL, is_active (toggle)
  * News: title, slug, featured image, summary, body (rich text), publish date, category (option), is_featured (toggle)
  * Insights: title, slug, featured image, summary, body (rich text), publish date, tags (multi-option), author, is_featured (toggle)
  * Industry Reports: title, slug, cover image, summary, file download URL, publish date, tags (multi-option), is_gated (toggle)
- [ ] Sample content added (3 items per collection) for template design testing
- [ ] CMS reference fields connected where applicable (e.g., Industry Reports tags linking to shared taxonomy)

**Core Symbols Built:**

- [ ] Header/Navigation symbol:
  * Logo (SVG), primary nav links, mobile hamburger trigger
  * Dropdown functionality (if in scope)
  * Sticky behavior configured
- [ ] Footer symbol:
  * All column links, copyright, social icons, legal links
  * Responsive layout (columns collapse to stack on mobile)
- [ ] Form components symbol:
  * Contact form (company, name, email, phone, industry, volume, message, submit)
  * Quote form (company, name, email, product type, volume, timeline, budget, notes)
  * All validation states styled (error, success, focus)
  * Form success messages configured
- [ ] CTA band symbol:
  * Heading, subtext, primary CTA button, optional secondary link
  * Background variants (light, dark, brand color)

**Utility Classes & Interactions Library:**

- [ ] Core utility classes built following Client-First:
  * Spacing utilities (u-margin-*, u-padding-*)
  * Text utilities (u-text-center, u-text-uppercase, etc.)
  * Layout utilities (u-container, u-grid-*, u-flex-*)
  * Visibility utilities (u-hide-tablet, u-hide-mobile)
- [ ] Interactions library configured:
  * Page load animation (subtle fade-in)
  * Scroll-triggered fade-in (for content sections)
  * Hover state transitions (buttons, cards, links)
  * Mobile menu open/close
  * Lottie animation triggers (for hero and icon animations from 2B.5)

- [ ] Symbols display correctly on Desktop, Tablet, Mobile breakpoints
- [ ] CMS sample content renders correctly in template views
- [ ] Interactions tested and working (no console errors)

Title: 4A.2: Performance Optimisation
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Optimise the full Webflow build to meet Core Web Vitals targets before launch. Run performance audits across key pages, identify bottlenecks, and implement fixes. Performance directly affects SEO rankings and user experience — the site must meet targets on mobile, which is harder to achieve and more impactful than desktop.

**Scope:**

* PageSpeed audits across all key page types (homepage, CMS templates, contact, solutions)
* Image optimisation pass (format, compression, lazy loading)
* Interaction and animation performance review
* Third-party script audit (analytics, embeds)
* Core Web Vitals confirmed within targets before launch

**Output:** All key pages meet or exceed performance targets. PageSpeed scores documented and shared with PM as pre-launch sign-off.

**Success Metric:**

* First Contentful Paint: <1.5s
* Largest Contentful Paint: <2.5s
* Total Blocking Time: <300ms
* Cumulative Layout Shift: <0.1
* PageSpeed Score: >90 mobile on homepage, >85 on CMS templates
* GTmetrix Grade: A

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Tools:**

* Google PageSpeed Insights: https://pagespeed.web.dev
* GTmetrix: https://gtmetrix.com
* Chrome DevTools Performance panel
* Webflow's built-in asset compression

**Common Performance Issues to Check:**

* Images not in WebP format or oversized (>200KB above fold, >150KB below fold)
* Render-blocking scripts (third-party tags loading synchronously)
* Lottie animations running on load when they should be deferred
* Unused CSS from Webflow's global stylesheet
* CLS caused by images without explicit width/height attributes
* Font loading causing layout shift (use font-display: swap)

**Key Pages to Audit (minimum):**

* Homepage (highest traffic, hero Lottie animation = risk)
* Solutions page (image-heavy)
* A CMS template page (News or Insights article)
* Contact / Get a Quote (form-heavy)
* Mobile performance is the primary target — desktop is secondary

**Priority Fix Order:**

1. Images above the fold (biggest LCP impact)
2. Render-blocking third-party scripts
3. CLS issues (layout shift)
4. Below-the-fold images (lazy loading)
5. Animation deferral

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Performance Audit:**

- [ ] PageSpeed Insights run on: Homepage, Solutions, a CMS template, Contact page
- [ ] GTmetrix run on Homepage
- [ ] All audit results documented (before and after scores)
- [ ] Issues prioritised by impact (P0: blocks target, P1: significant improvement, P2: minor)

**Image Optimisation:**

- [ ] All images verified in WebP format
- [ ] Hero/above-fold images <200KB
- [ ] Content images <150KB
- [ ] All images have explicit width and height attributes (prevents CLS)
- [ ] Below-the-fold images confirmed lazy loading
- [ ] Retina/2x images only where necessary (not applied site-wide)

**Script & Asset Optimisation:**

- [ ] Third-party scripts (GA4, any embeds) loading asynchronously
- [ ] No render-blocking scripts in <head> without async/defer
- [ ] Unused CSS reviewed (Webflow limitations noted where applicable)
- [ ] Font loading strategy confirmed (font-display: swap or equivalent)

**Animation Performance:**

- [ ] Hero Lottie animation: file size confirmed <100KB, deferred until after page load
- [ ] Scroll-triggered animations not causing layout shift
- [ ] Interactions tested on low-end mobile simulation (Chrome DevTools throttling)

**Core Web Vitals Targets Met:**

- [ ] FCP <1.5s on Homepage (mobile)
- [ ] LCP <2.5s on Homepage (mobile)
- [ ] TBT <300ms on Homepage (mobile)
- [ ] CLS <0.1 on Homepage (mobile)
- [ ] PageSpeed mobile score >90 on Homepage
- [ ] PageSpeed mobile score >85 on CMS template page
- [ ] GTmetrix Grade A on Homepage

**Documentation:**

- [ ] Before/after PageSpeed scores saved: \[screenshots\]
- [ ] Any scores that could not meet target: documented with reason and accepted by PM
- [ ] Performance summary ready for PM sign-off

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

## Task Dropoff

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders when all the acceptance criteria is completed. If needed, mark the task as **In Review**.*

* GTmetrix report (Homepage): \[link or screenshot\]

  * FCP, LCP, TBT, CLS, PageSpeed score — before and after
* Any targets not met: documented with reason and PM acknowledgement
* Confirmation: "Performance targets met, ready for launch"

**Mark task as "In Review"** and tag PM to confirm performance sign-off. Can run in parallel with 4A.1 UAT — does not need to wait for client approval.

# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Complete all pre-launch tasks to ensure a clean, zero-downtime go-live. This includes the final pre-launch checklist, domain and DNS preparation, redirect configuration, Webflow publish settings, and coordinating the go-live window with the client. Nothing goes live until every item on the pre-launch checklist is complete and PM has given the green light.

**Scope:**

* Pre-launch checklist completed across all categories (SEO, forms, analytics, legal, accessibility)
* Domain and DNS prepared (client to action DNS change at agreed time)
* 301 redirects configured and tested (if replacing existing site)
* Webflow project settings finalised (custom domain, favicon, 404 page, indexing)
* Go-live window agreed with client and team

**Output:** Site ready to publish to production. All checklist items complete. Domain change prepared and client briefed on their action (DNS update). Team aligned on go-live window.

**Success Metric:** Go-live completes without issues. Site live within agreed window. No broken redirects, no indexing errors, no downtime.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

* UAT approval documentation (4A.1): \[link\]
* Performance sign-off (4A.2): \[link\]

**Pre-Launch Checklist Categories:**

* SEO: meta tags, Open Graph, schema, sitemap, robots.txt, canonicals
* Analytics: GA4 tracking verified, conversion events confirmed, Search Console live
* Forms: all 4 forms tested end-to-end, submission routing confirmed
* Legal: Privacy Policy and Terms of Use published with client copy (not placeholder)
* Accessibility: WCAG AA spot-check on key pages
* Content: no placeholder text, all images have alt text, all links working
* Technical: favicon set, 404 configured, indexing enabled (not blocked), SSL active
* Redirects: 301 redirect map implemented and tested (if replacing existing site)

**Domain Setup Process:**

* Custom domain added to Webflow project settings
* Client updates DNS records at their registrar (A record or CNAME)
* SSL certificate issued automatically by Webflow after DNS propagation (up to 48 hours)
* Do not publish to production until SSL is confirmed active

**Go-Live Window:**

* Agree with client on preferred go-live date and time
* Recommend: low-traffic period (early morning, weekday)
* Have team available during the window (1-2 hours) to monitor and fix any immediate issues

SEO:

- [ ] XML sitemap accessible at /sitemap.xml

- [ ] Schema markup validated (no errors in Rich Results Test)

Analytics:

Technical:

- [ ] Custom 404 page set in Webflow project settings
- [ ] Site indexing enabled in Webflow (not set to "No index")
- [ ] SSL certificate active on production domain
- [ ] All internal links working (no 404s — final link check)

Redirects (if applicable):

- [ ] 301 redirect list implemented in Webflow
- [ ] Redirects tested: old URLs resolve to correct new URLs
- [ ] No redirect chains (A → B → C) — all go direct

**Domain & DNS Preparation:**

Title: 4A.7: CSAT & Review Collection
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

\[Note: Check with commercial team on how to collect the CSAT survey\]

Collect client satisfaction feedback via CSAT survey, and if the score is 9 or above, request a Google review. This is the final task of the engagement and directly impacts RMD's reputation and new business pipeline. The process is time-sensitive — initiate within 24 hours of handover while satisfaction is highest.

**Scope:**

* CSAT survey sent and response collected
* If CSAT ≥9: Google review requested with direct link and talking points
* If CSAT <9: concerns addressed before requesting public review
* Clutch submission initiated (Week 2 post-launch)
* Engagement formally closed

**Output:** CSAT score documented. Google review secured (if CSAT ≥9). Clutch submission initiated. Engagement closed.

**Success Metric:** CSAT score ≥9. Google review published within 2 weeks of launch. Clutch submission submitted within 30 days.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Reference Materials:**

* CSAT survey template: \[link\]
* Google review direct link: \[link — RMD Google Business Profile\]
* Clutch profile: \[link\]

**CSAT Survey Questions (example):**

* Overall satisfaction with the project: 1-10
* Quality of design and visual output: 1-10
* Quality of build and technical delivery: 1-10
* Communication and project management: 1-10
* Would you recommend RMD to others? Yes / No
* Open feedback: What went well? What could we improve?

**Review Collection Sequence:**

* Post-handover (within 24 hours): Send CSAT survey
* If CSAT ≥9 (within 48-72 hours): Request Google review with direct link and suggested talking points
* If no review after 7 days: Follow up once (not multiple times)
* 2 weeks post-launch: Initiate Clutch submission — schedule client interview
* 30 days post-launch: Clutch submission complete

**Google Review Talking Points (to share with client):**

* The project goals and what was delivered
* The quality of the design and build
* The team's communication and responsiveness
* What they would say to another company considering working with RMD
* (Keep it natural — do not script word-for-word)

**If CSAT <9:**

* Do not request a public review
* Schedule a call to understand concerns
* Address any outstanding issues within agreed scope
* Reassess after concerns resolved
* Escalate to leadership if CSAT <7

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**CSAT Survey:**

- [ ] CSAT survey sent to client sponsor and PM within 24 hours of handover
- [ ] Response received within 48 hours (chase once if no response)
- [ ] CSAT score documented internally
- [ ] Open feedback notes saved to project folder
- [ ] Score shared with PM and leadership

**If CSAT ≥9 — Google Review:**

- [ ] Google review request sent (direct link + suggested talking points)
- [ ] Timing: within 72 hours of positive CSAT score
- [ ] Review published: confirmed and saved (screenshot)
- [ ] If no review after 7 days: one follow-up sent

**If CSAT <9 — Issue Resolution:**

- [ ] Call scheduled with client to understand concerns
- [ ] Outstanding issues documented and actioned within scope
- [ ] Escalated to leadership if CSAT <7
- [ ] Public review not requested until concerns resolved

**Clutch Submission:**

- [ ] Clutch submission process initiated (2 weeks post-launch)
- [ ] Client interview scheduled
- [ ] Client briefed on Clutch process
- [ ] Submission completed within 30 days of launch

**Engagement Close:**

- [ ] CSAT score and review outcome documented in project folder
- [ ] Google review link saved (if received)
- [ ] Clutch submission status noted
- [ ] Engagement formally marked closed in Linear and internally

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* CSAT response not received — chase once after 48 hours, escalate to account if still no response
* CSAT <9 and client has unresolved concerns — do not request public review, escalate to leadership immediately
* Client unclear on how to leave a Google review — offer to walk them through it on a quick call

# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Build all pages under the Contact Us section: the Contact Us overview, Get a Quote, Book a Factory Tour, and Request Samples. This section is the primary conversion hub for Hop Lun's commercial objectives. Every form must work correctly end-to-end — submissions must reach the right recipient and trigger confirmation messages.

**Scope:**

* 4 pages: Contact Us, Get a Quote, Book a Factory Tour, Request Samples
* All forms built and tested (form submissions, validation, success states)
* Form submission routing confirmed with client (email destination or CRM)

**Output:** All 4 pages built, responsive, all forms tested end-to-end (submission → confirmation email or success state), published to staging.

**Success Metric:** Every form submits successfully in staging, required field validation works, success messages display correctly. Form submissions reach the correct email address.

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

* Form components symbol (built in 3A.1c): \[Webflow link\]

* **Contact Us:** name, company, email, phone, message — general enquiry
* **Get a Quote:** name, company, email, product type, volume, timeline, budget, notes
* **Book a Factory Tour:** name, company, email, preferred date, group size, notes
* **Request Samples:** name, company, email, product type, sample quantity, shipping address

**Form Submission Routing:**

* Confirm with PM/client where each form submission should be sent (email address or CRM/webhook)
* Webflow native form submissions can forward to email — set up in Webflow form settings
* If CRM integration required (HubSpot, Salesforce etc.) — flag to PM as this may require Zapier or custom integration and additional scoping

**Validation Rules:**

* Required fields: all name, company, email fields are required minimum
* Email format validation: enforced on all email fields

* Date field (Tour): must be a future date (if date picker is used)

This task runs in parallel with page builds and should be completed before UAT begins.

**Scope:**

* SEO: meta titles and descriptions for all pages, Open Graph tags, schema markup, XML sitemap, robots.txt
* Analytics: GA4 implementation, event tracking for key conversions (form submissions, CTA clicks)
* Google Search Console: property verified and sitemap submitted

**Output:** All pages have SEO meta data. Analytics tracking verified via GA4 DebugView. Search Console property live with sitemap submitted.

**Success Metric:** GA4 DebugView shows page views and form submission events firing correctly on staging. PageSpeed score >85 on mobile (pre-launch baseline).

Title: 3A.11: Responsive QA & Cross-Browser Testing
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Conduct full site QA across all pages, breakpoints, and browsers before handing off to UAT. This is the internal quality gate that catches issues before the client sees them. Every page is checked for responsiveness, visual accuracy, interaction behaviour, broken links, and performance. Issues are logged in Linear with severity tags and resolved before UAT begins.

**Scope:**

* All pages across 3 breakpoints (Desktop 1440px, Tablet 768px, Mobile 375px)
* Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge — desktop and mobile)
* All interactions and animations verified
* All forms tested end-to-end
* All CMS templates tested with sample content
* Performance checks across key pages

**Output:** QA report with all issues logged and resolved. No P0 or P1 issues remaining. Staging confirmed ready for client UAT.

**Success Metric:** <5 P0/P1 issues found (indicating quality build). All P0/P1 issues resolved before UAT. PM sign-off: "Staging is ready for client."

## Additional Notes

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

**Reference Materials:**

* All approved Figma designs (Phase 2B): \[Figma link\]
* All staging page URLs: \[staging URL — full site\]
* QA checklist template: \[Google Drive link\]

**Pages to QA (full site):**

* Home
* About Us, Leadership, Our Story, Locations (listing + template)
* Solutions, Design Studio, Material Innovation, Product Development, Manufacturing
* Sustainability, Environmental, Social Responsibility
* Careers (listing + template)
* Resources & Insights hub, News (listing + template), Insights (listing + template), Industry Reports (listing + template)
* Contact Us, Get a Quote, Book a Factory Tour, Request Samples
* Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, 404

**Browsers to Test:**

* Chrome (latest, Desktop + Mobile/Chrome DevTools)
* Safari (latest, Desktop + iOS)
* Firefox (latest, Desktop)
* Edge (latest, Desktop)

**Issue Severity:**

* P0 (Critical): Broken layout, broken form, missing page, 404 error, JavaScript error
* P1 (High): Visual deviation >20px from design, interaction not working, CTA not linked
* P2 (Medium): Minor spacing inconsistency, small copy error, minor visual tweak
* P3 (Low): Micro-enhancement, future improvement, out-of-scope polish

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Responsiveness (All Pages):**

- [ ] All pages verified at Desktop (1440px): no overflow, no broken layouts
- [ ] All pages verified at Tablet (768px): layout adapts correctly
- [ ] All pages verified at Mobile (375px): layout stacks correctly, text readable, tap targets ≥44px
- [ ] No horizontal scroll on any page at any breakpoint

**Visual Accuracy (Key Pages):**

- [ ] Homepage checked against Figma Desktop frame
- [ ] Solutions pages checked against Figma
- [ ] Contact section pages checked against Figma
- [ ] All deviations documented and triaged (P0/P1 fixed, P2/P3 logged)

**Interactions & Animations:**

- [ ] Scroll-triggered animations working on all pages
- [ ] Hero Lottie animation playing correctly (Homepage)
- [ ] Mobile menu open/close working across browsers
- [ ] All hover states working (buttons, cards, nav links)
- [ ] No JavaScript errors in browser console (all browsers)

**Forms:**

- [ ] All 4 forms submit successfully in staging (end-to-end tested)
- [ ] Required field validation working on all forms
- [ ] Success messages displaying correctly
- [ ] Form submissions reaching correct email address (confirmed in 3A.8 — re-verify here)

**CMS Templates:**

- [ ] Locations template tested with all sample items
- [ ] Careers template tested with all sample items
- [ ] News, Insights, Industry Reports templates tested with all sample items
- [ ] is_active toggle: inactive Careers items not appearing on listing
- [ ] is_gated toggle: gated Industry Reports triggering form gate correctly
- [ ] is_featured: featured items appearing on Resources hub page

**Links & Navigation:**

- [ ] All internal links working (no 404s)
- [ ] All CTA buttons linked to correct destinations
- [ ] Footer links all working (including Privacy, Terms, social icons)
- [ ] Nav dropdowns linking to correct sub-pages
- [ ] 404 page triggering on invalid URLs

**Performance:**

- [ ] PageSpeed score checked on Homepage: target >85 mobile, >90 desktop
- [ ] PageSpeed score checked on a CMS template: target >80 mobile
- [ ] Images lazy-loading below the fold on all key pages
- [ ] No render-blocking resources flagged as critical

**Issue Resolution:**

- [ ] All P0 issues resolved and re-tested
- [ ] All P1 issues resolved and re-tested
- [ ] P2/P3 issues logged in Linear for post-launch or acknowledged as accepted risk
- [ ] QA summary report created: total issues, breakdown by severity, pages with most issues

* Consistency (navigation, footer, CTAs, forms)
* Responsive variants (Desktop 1440px, Tablet 768px, Mobile 375px)
* Conversion paths (CTAs present and positioned correctly)
* Content accuracy (placeholder copy, form fields, legal pages)
* Brand alignment (colors, positioning, tone)

**Severity Definitions:**

* **P0 (Critical):** Blocks client review - broken flow, missing pages, major inconsistency
* **P1 (High):** Should fix before client - CTA missing, form field wrong, pattern broken
* **P2 (Medium):** Nice to fix - minor spacing, copy tweak, small inconsistency
* **P3 (Low):** Future consideration - enhancement, out of scope

**Tools:**

* Figma for visual review
* Linear for issue logging
* Responsive preview (Figma or export to prototype)

**Reference Materials:**

* Design patterns doc from 2A.2: \[link\]
* Brand guidelines: `/01_CLIENT_PROVIDED/Brand Assets/`
* Conversion strategy from 2A.1: 3 CTAs (Download Profile, Get Quote, Contact)

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Consistency Check:**

- [ ] Navigation structure consistent across all 8-15 pages (header, mobile hamburger)
- [ ] Footer structure matches across all pages (columns, links, social, legal)
- [ ] CTA styles consistent (primary, secondary, positioning)
- [ ] Form layouts follow defined patterns
- [ ] Spacing and typography hierarchy consistent

**Responsive Variants:**

- [ ] All pages verified at Desktop (1440px) breakpoint
- [ ] All pages verified at Tablet (768px) breakpoint
- [ ] All pages verified at Mobile (375px) breakpoint
- [ ] Interactive elements (nav, forms, CTAs) work at all breakpoints
- [ ] Content doesn't break or overflow at any breakpoint

**Conversion Path Check:**

- [ ] Homepage has primary CTAs (Download Profile, Get Quote)
- [ ] All relevant pages have conversion CTAs positioned strategically
- [ ] Contact page has proper B2B form fields (company name, industry, volume, timeline)
- [ ] Get Quote page has lead capture form
- [ ] Footer CTAs present on all pages

**Content Accuracy:**

- [ ] Placeholder copy matches brand positioning ("modern manufacturing leader")
- [ ] Key stats accurate (150+ designers, 150M+ pieces, 30 years)
- [ ] Form field labels correct (not generic Relume placeholders)
- [ ] Legal pages present (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service)
- [ ] No lorem ipsum or default Relume copy remaining

**Brand Alignment:**

- [ ] Brand colors used correctly (Primary #FFB2E0, Secondary #F1D66F, Tertiary #F8F1EB, Neutral #A5AAB8)
- [ ] Tone matches defined strategy (professional but modern, approachable not stuffy)
- [ ] Positioning clear (differentiated from Cerie, Mas, Crystal Martin - traditional competitors)

**Issue Logging:**

- [ ] All issues logged in Linear with HPL-2A3 parent task
- [ ] Each issue has severity tag (P0, P1, P2, P3)
- [ ] Each issue has clear description + screenshot + page location
- [ ] Issues assigned back to Tiara or Mona for fix (if fixing before client review)
- [ ] QA report summary created: Total issues, breakdown by severity, top 3 concerns

**Quality Target:**

- [ ] <5 P0/P1 issues found (indicates quality refinement in 2A.2)
- [ ] All P0 issues flagged immediately to Tiara/Shafiq
- [ ] QA complete and ready for client review coordination (2A.4)

## Task Blockers

**Tag** @tiara **if unclear what qualifies as an issue. Tag** @shafiq **if pattern deviation is major enough to delay client review. Emergency escalations:** @sean **(design) or** @davis **(technical) if Shafiq unavailable.**

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* Too many P0/P1 issues found (>10) - may need to loop back to 2A.2 refinement
* Missing pages or major structural issues - escalate to @tiara immediately
* Can't verify responsive behavior in Figma - export prototype or ask @tiara

Title: 2A.4: Client Review Coordination
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Coordinate wireframe review with client stakeholders. Prepare materials, schedule review session, collect feedback, and log all items in Linear for processing. Shafiq or Rohan owns this coordination task to ensure smooth client engagement and clear feedback collection.

**Work Structure:**

* **Shafiq or Rohan (4 pts):** Client coordination, materials prep, feedback collection

**Output:** Client feedback collected, logged in Linear, categorized by priority, ready for amendment processing.

**Success Metric:** Client review conducted within agreed timeline, all feedback logged within 24 hours, <5 in-scope change requests.

## Additional Notes

> *List any additional resources/materials here that would be helpful to complete the task*

**Review Materials to Prepare:**

* Wireframes exported as PDF or interactive Figma prototype
* Presentation deck (optional - if formal review)
* Review link with view-only access (Figma)

**Client Stakeholders:**

* Client Sponsor: \[NAME\] - Strategic approval
* Client PM: \[NAME\] - Day-to-day feedback
* Other reviewers: \[LIST if known\]

**Feedback Deadline:**

* Set clear deadline: 3 business days from review session
* Communicate: "Feedback needed by \[DATE\] to stay on timeline"

**Review Session Format:**

* Duration: 60 mins
* Agenda: IA walkthrough, key pages review, Q&A, next steps
* Platform: Zoom/Teams with screen share or in-person

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Preparation:**

- [ ] All 8-15 wireframes exported as PDF or interactive prototype link
- [ ] Figma link prepared with view-only access for client
- [ ] Presentation deck created (if needed for formal review)
- [ ] Review link/materials sent to client stakeholders 2 days before session
- [ ] Review session scheduled with all key stakeholders confirmed

**Review Session:**

- [ ] Session conducted (60 mins): IA walkthrough, key pages, Q&A
- [ ] Key decisions documented during call
- [ ] Questions answered or flagged for follow-up
- [ ] Feedback deadline communicated (3 business days)
- [ ] Next steps confirmed (amendments timeline, design phase start)

**Feedback Collection:**

- [ ] All client feedback collected via agreed method (email, Figma comments, doc)
- [ ] Feedback logged in Linear within 24 hours
- [ ] Each feedback item categorized:
  * In-scope vs Out-of-scope
  * P0 (blocker) vs P1 (important) vs P2 (nice-to-have)
- [ ] Out-of-scope items flagged for change request discussion
- [ ] Scope >5% increase flagged to Shafiq for change order

**Communication:**

- [ ] Feedback summary shared with Tiara + Mona
- [ ] Timeline impact assessed (if any major changes)
- [ ] Client confirmation sent: "Received feedback, processing in 2A.5"
- [ ] Internal team briefed on amendment priorities

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Tag** @tiara **if client feedback requires design clarification. Tag** @shafiq **or** @rohan **for scope questions or timeline concerns. Escalate to leadership if client requests major scope change (>20%).**

**Common blockers to watch for:**

* Client slow to schedule review - chase 1 week before target date
* Client feedback unclear or contradictory - schedule follow-up call
* Major scope change requested - pause amendments, discuss change order first
* Key stakeholder missing from review - reschedule or get async approval

  * In-scope: X items (P0: X, P1: X, P2: X)
  * Out-of-scope: X items (requires change request)
* Timeline impact assessment: "On track" or "Delayed by X days due to..."
* Client confirmation email sent: \[link or screenshot\]

**Mark task as "Complete"** and trigger 2A.5 Process Amendments. If major scope change, pause and discuss change order before proceeding.

Title: 2A.5: Process Amendments
Description:
# Task Overview

> *Add a description to help describe what you are achieving for the project.*

Process all client feedback from wireframe review. Tiara handles structural changes and complex revisions. Mona handles minor copy/layout changes. Prioritize P0/P1 feedback, log P2/P3 for future or mark out-of-scope. Get final client approval and lock wireframes before starting design phase.

**Work Structure:**

* **Tiara (8 pts):** Structural changes, complex revisions, major repositioning
* **Mona (4 pts):** Minor copy changes, layout tweaks, simple adjustments

**Output:** All P0/P1 client feedback addressed, wireframes approved and locked by client, ready for design phase handoff.

**Success Metric:** Client formal approval received (email or signed-off doc), wireframes locked (no more structural changes), Gate G2 criteria met.

## Additional Notes

> *List any additional resources/materials here that would be helpful to complete the task*

**Feedback from 2A.4:**

* Client feedback log in Linear: \[link\]
* Categorized feedback (In-scope P0/P1/P2, Out-of-scope): \[link\]

**Amendment Scope Definitions:**

* **Structural (Tiara):** Page reordering, navigation changes, major content restructuring, new sections
* **Minor (Mona):** Copy edits, button repositioning, spacing adjustments, form field tweaks

**Gate G2 Criteria (Must achieve):**

* All 8-15 wireframes designed and refined
* QA completed with <5 P0/P1 issues
* Client review conducted
* All P0/P1 feedback addressed
* Client formal approval received
* Wireframes locked (no more structural changes after this)

**Out-of-Scope Process:**

* P2 feedback that adds >5% work â†' Log for post-launch or decline
* Requests for new pages â†' Issue change request
* Requests beyond original SOW â†' Escalate to Shafiq/Rohan for change order

## Acceptance Criteria

> *List the requirements that must be met for completion approval, if any below*

**Tiara's Amendment Scope (8 pts):**

- [ ] All P0 structural changes implemented (if any)
- [ ] All P1 structural changes implemented
- [ ] Major content repositioning complete
- [ ] Navigation changes applied (if requested)
- [ ] New sections added (if in-scope)
- [ ] Complex revisions requiring design judgment complete
- [ ] Reviewed all Mona's changes for consistency

**Mona's Amendment Scope (4 pts):**

- [ ] All P0 minor changes implemented (copy, layout, spacing)
- [ ] All P1 minor changes implemented
- [ ] Copy edits applied across relevant pages
- [ ] Form field adjustments made
- [ ] Button/CTA repositioning complete (if minor)
- [ ] Spacing and alignment tweaks done

**P2/P3 Feedback Handling:**

- [ ] All P2 feedback logged in Linear with "Future" or "Out-of-Scope" label
- [ ] P2 items requiring >2 points flagged to Shafiq (potential change request)
- [ ] P3 feedback acknowledged but marked "Out-of-Scope" in Linear
- [ ] Client informed which P2/P3 items will not be included (with reasoning)

**Final Approval:**

- [ ] All P0/P1 changes implemented and reviewed
- [ ] Updated wireframes exported for final client review
- [ ] Final approval session scheduled (if needed) or async approval requested
- [ ] Client formal approval received (email or signed doc)
- [ ] Wireframes marked as "Locked" in Figma (no more changes allowed)
- [ ] Gate G2 checklist complete and documented

**Handoff Preparation:**

- [ ] Wireframes exported for development team reference
- [ ] Amendment log finalized (what changed from original)
- [ ] Design phase kickoff scheduled
- [ ] Team briefed: "Wireframes locked, design phase starts next cycle"

## Task Blockers

> *Drop the important links to your tasks and work below and notify your team leaders or members when you face a blocker that needs to be review. Mark the task as **Blocker**.*

**Tag** @tiara **for complex change questions. Tag** @shafiq **if scope >5% increase or timeline at risk. Tag** @rohan **if client approval delayed. Emergency escalations:** @sean **(design) or** @davis **(technical) if Shafiq unavailable.**

**Common blockers to watch for:**
